name: Fenghuang
primary_sources: Silk Painting of Human Figure with Dragon and Phoenix

visual_attributes.morphology: Composite bird with features from various animals
distinctive_features: Tail of a fish or peacock
visual_attributes.texture: Feathery with smooth and scaly elements
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Large bird, comparable to a peacock
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Head of a golden pheasant, body of a mandarin duck, tail of a peacock, legs of a crane, mouth of a parrot, wings of a swallow

domain_specific_visual.SYMBOLIZES_CELESTIAL_BODIES: Head is the sky, eyes are the sun, back is the moon, wings are the wind, feet are the earth, tail is the planets
domain_specific_visual.CARRIES_ITEMS: Scrolls or a box with sacred books, sometimes depicted with a fireball

secondary_functions: Symbol of union of yin and yang

domain_specific_functional.APPEARS_IN_PEACEFUL_PLACES: Only appears in areas blessed with utmost peace and prosperity
domain_specific_functional.REPRESENTS_CONFUCIAN_VIRTUES: Represents benevolence, honesty, knowledge, faithfulness, and propriety

relationships.description: Represents the feminine counterpart to the masculine dragon

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents imperial power, virtue, and the union of yin and yang
